subject: Boehme / Böhme family line
connection_to_hunter: Hilda Boehme → Jennive Imogene Lepick → Shari Mattingly → Hunter Spence

primary_source_anchor.text: Jennive Imogene Lepick was born on February 2, 1923 in Floresville, Texas to Frederick and Hilda (Boehme) Lepick

birth.note: Inferred from sibling sequence; all siblings born TX; Herman (father) died in Shiner 1900

father.name: Unknown German immigrant
father.note: Primary research gap. Estimated arrival ~1855-1862 via Galveston or Indianola. 1870/1880 Lavaca County census records are recommended next step.

evidence: Buried at Sons of Hermann Cemetery (German Protestant fraternal organization, founded San Antonio 1861)
Child Frieda Boehme buried at Saint Johns Lutheran Cemetery, Poth, Wilson County TX

children.name: Frieda Boehme
children.burial: Saint Johns Lutheran Cemetery, Poth, Wilson County TX

children.name: Edgar Charles Boehme
children.burial: Shiner Catholic Cemetery, Shiner, Lavaca County TX
children.note: Catholic burial anomalous vs. Lutheran family pattern; possibly converted via marriage

children.name: Dora Boehme
children.burial: Floresville City Cemetery, Wilson County TX

children.name: Walter John Boehme
children.burial: Shiner Sons of Herman Cemetery, Lavaca County TX

children.name: Annie Henrietta Boehme
children.burial: Floresville City Cemetery, Wilson County TX

children.name: Hilda Boehme (Lepick)
children.burial: Floresville City Cemetery, Wilson County TX

immigration.value: Hilda was native-born Texas; immigrant generation was Herman's parents (G6), ~1855-1862

Q2_settlement_region.region: Lavaca County / Wilson County, South Texas German Belt
Q2_settlement_region.not_adelsverein: Confirmed — Adelsverein settlements were Hill Country (Gillespie/Kendall/Comal counties). Lavaca/Wilson County is a distinct immigration corridor.

Q3_immigration_wave.reasoning: Herman born 1863 TX; Lavaca County not an Adelsverein settlement zone; entry via Galveston or Indianola consistent with 1850s-1860s German migration corridor to South Texas

Q4_notable_boehmes.assessment: No notable Boehme figures identified in Lavaca/Wilson County records or TSHA Handbook
Q4_notable_boehmes.note: Working-class/small-farming immigrant family consistent with Sons of Hermann demographic

evidence: Herman buried Sons of Hermann Cemetery (German Protestant fraternal)
Frieda Boehme (Herman's child) buried Saint Johns Lutheran Cemetery, Poth TX
Q5_church_affiliation.anomaly: Edgar Boehme (sibling) buried Shiner Catholic Cemetery — individual divergence, does not negate family Lutheran pattern

research_gaps.gap: Herman F. Boehme's parents (G6) are unidentified
next_steps: 1870 US Federal Census, Lavaca County TX (Herman age ~7; NARA M593, roll 1595)
1880 US Federal Census, Lavaca County TX (Herman age ~17; NARA T9 Texas schedules)
Texas Death Certificate for Herman 1900 — note: TX statewide registration began 1903; county-level records at Lavaca County Courthouse
St. Paul Lutheran Church records, Shiner TX
Sons of Hermann lodge membership records, Grand Lodge of Texas, San Antonio
German immigration manifests, Galveston/Indianola 1855-1862 (Genealogy.net Galveston lists)
Herman F. Boehme wife may appear in 1900 census (died same year as Herman — possibly together or shortly after)
Wilson County marriage records for Frederick Lepick + Hilda Boehme

settlement_context.contrast: NOT Hill Country Adelsverein zone (Fredericksburg/New Braunfels)
settlement_context.fraternal: Sons of Hermann (Söhne Hermanns), founded San Antonio 1861
settlement_context.religion: Lutheran (dominant German Protestant tradition in this corridor)
